title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains a compound called gallic acid, which is known for its ability to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in the eyes. When applied topically or taken as a supplement, witch hazel tincture can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup, resulting in a significant decrease in eye swelling. This natural remedy can provide fast and effective relief from puffy, swollen eyes caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other conditions.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: The tannins present in Witch Hazel help constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and swelling around the eyes by limiting fluid leakage.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in Witch Hazel, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and swelling.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The bioactive compounds in Witch Hazel, including lignans and triterpenoids, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eye issues.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: A polyphenolic compound that acts as an astringent, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation around the eyes by constricting blood vessels and preventing excessive fluid accumulation.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the swelling and redness.

Gallic acid: A phenolic acid that exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help reduce eye swelling by scavenging free radicals, inhibiting inflammatory mediators, and promoting the breakdown of excess fluids and waste products.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties to reduce swelling and soothe the affected area.
Barks: Utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area around the eyes.
Stems: Employed for their anti-inflammatory properties to help alleviate eye swelling and promote a calming effect.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 1 week.
Chop 1/2 cup of the dried plant material into small pieces.
Steep 1/2 cup of the chopped plant material in 2 cups of 80 proof vodka for 4 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, specifically targeting the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. The tannins present in the herb have been shown to reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from further damage caused by free radicals. As a result, this natural remedy can effectively alleviate eye swelling and promote a more refreshed and youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula's flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus helping to alleviate eye swelling.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Pot Marigold tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, promoting healthy tissue function and reducing damage to the eyes.

Vasodilatory: Calendula's flavonoids also exhibit vasodilatory effects, which means they help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing congestion and inflammation in the eyes that can lead to swelling.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Flavonoids: Act as potent antioxidants, neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress that contributes to eye inflammation and swelling.
Naphthoquinones (e.g., plumbagin): Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, inhibiting the release of inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to eye swelling and redness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling and promote healing.
Leaves: Utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, making them effective in treating eye swelling.
Stems: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and preventing infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the fresh calendula flowers in a single layer in a warm dark place for 1 to 2 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried calendula flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids to obtain the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the plant's active compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin. These natural flavonoids work to reduce puffiness and redness by calming irritated tissues and soothing swollen eyelids. The tincture also contains antihistamine-like compounds that help alleviate itchiness and discharge associated with eye allergies or irritations. As a result, Kansas coneflower tinctures provide effective relief from uncomfortable and unsightly eye swelling, allowing for clear and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Kansas Coneflower's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to eye swelling.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Kansas Coneflower have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes.
Immunomodulatory properties: Kansas Coneflower has been shown to modulate the immune response, which can help prevent excessive inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, specifically echinacoside, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate eye discomfort.
Phenolic Acids (such as chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.

Alkamides (such as cis- and trans-11, 13-dihydro-elephantopin): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help to regulate the immune response and reduce eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are used due to their high concentration of compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm eye swelling.

Roots is repeated here. However,  Rhyzomes is also used. Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used due to their ability to inhibit the release of histamine and other inflammatory compounds that cause eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in early spring or late fall.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a dark glass jar for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to clean dark glass bottles.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with eye swelling because its extract contains flavonoids, which possess potent anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation in the body is particularly beneficial for alleviating eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or minor injuries. As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, it works by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals responsible for causing blood vessels to dilate and lead to swelling. This natural remedy promotes healthy vision and provides relief from discomfort and puffiness associated with eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye swelling.
Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures can dilate blood vessels, reducing swelling by improving circulation and reducing fluid buildup around the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains anti-inflammatory saponins that may help reduce inflammation and edema in the eye tissues, alleviating swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to eye swelling.
Bilobalide: This triterpene acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eye, thereby alleviating swelling.
Quercetin: As a powerful flavonoid, quercetin possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the swelling and inflammation associated with eye conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used for making tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for the medicinal effects.
Seeds: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with eye swelling.
Barks: They are also used to make tinctures due to their flavonoid content, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers and combine them with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add a small amount of glycerin to the liquid and mix well to enhance the tincture's shelf life.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place, away from children and pets, for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in various parts of the body, including the eyes. When applied topically as an eye drop or added to a warm compress, St John's Wort tincture can help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing puffiness and discomfort associated with eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: St. John's Wort contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort tinctures help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of damage to the delicate tissues of the eye.

Vasodilatory effects: The bioactive compounds in St. John's Wort tinctures may help dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing congestion in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic compound that exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce eye swelling by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and scavenging free radicals.

Quercetin: A flavonoid that is a known anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which may help alleviate eye swelling by inhibiting histamine release, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating inflammatory pathways.

N-phenyl-1-naphthylamine: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help mitigate eye swelling by suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory cytokines and scavenging free radicals.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in hyperoside, an anti-inflammatory flavonoid that helps reduce swelling.
Flowers: High in hypericin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate eye swelling.
Barks: Contains a mixture of flavonoids, including quercetin, that have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of the fresh flowering tops of hypericum perforatum in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 100g of the dried plant material with 500ml of 80% ethanol.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with eye swelling because it possesses anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The aloe vera gel, when extracted and concentrated into a tincture form, can be easily absorbed into the skin around the eyes, targeting the delicate orbital area to calm and decongest blood vessels. This natural remedy gently relieves eye strain and discomfort, leaving the eyes feeling refreshed, revitalized, and free from swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by suppressing pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antioxidant: Aloe's antioxidant properties, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and puffiness.
Soften and calm irritated tissues: Aloe tincture's mucilages and polysaccharides soothe and soften irritated tissues in the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort associated with swelling.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These glycosidic compounds help reduce eye swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues.
Phenolic acids: Compounds like salicylic acid and ferulic acid present in Aloe vera exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help alleviate eye swelling and promote faster healing.

Aloe-emodin: A chromone-derived anthraquinone, aloe-emodin has been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and anti-edematous properties, effectively reducing eye swelling and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling and ease eye irritation.
Gel from Leaves: The clear, gel-like substance found inside the Aloe vera leaf is rich in hyaluronic acid, glycoproteins, and other soothing compounds that help calm and moisturize the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-25 aloe vera leaves with thick fleshy skin from mature plants during summer months when the gel is most potent.
Wash the leaves under running water to remove dirt and other impurities from the skin.
Slice the leaves into thin pieces and soak them in a 1:1 ratio of aloe vera to 80 proof ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow the aloe vera to infuse into the ethanol slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and soothe irritated eyes. The root extract of burdock contains powerful antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow to the delicate tissues around the eyes. Additionally, burdock's gentle diuretic properties help to drain excess fluids from the body, further alleviating water retention and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes by reducing inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in burdock tincture can help neutralize free radicals that may be contributing to eye swelling, promoting overall eye health and protecting against damage.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Burdock's astringent and antiseptic compounds can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection by constricting blood vessels and killing bacteria or other microorganisms in the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes due to their potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Terpenoids: These compounds, particularly arctiin, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.

Saponins: As a type of triterpenoid saponin, arctigenin in Arctium lappa has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help mitigate eye swelling and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used because they contain a high concentration of inulin and other anti-inflammatory compounds.
Seeds: Seeds are used because they contain a high amount of sesquiterpene lactones, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they contain various flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctium lappa roots in the spring or fall when they are mature and dry them immediately.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and measure out 1 part roots to 2 parts menstruum such as vodka.
Combine the chopped roots and vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using it to allow the flavors to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's high concentration of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and saponins, work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the eyes. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and other ocular conditions characterized by swelling, redness, and discharge. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds present in horsetail tincture neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation, protecting the delicate tissues of the eyes and reducing oxidative stress.

remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Equisetum arvense have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation around the eyes.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Equisetum arvense, including equisetic and isoequisetic, may exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce eye swelling and promote recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Equisetum arvense roots are used due to their high content of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling.
Leaves: Equisetum arvense leaves are used for their cooling and soothing effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Stems: Equisetum arvense stems are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote drainage, which can help to alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense stems and roots in the spring or fall season.
Chop the harvested equisetum arvense material into small pieces and weigh out 250 grams.
Combine the chopped equisetum arvense with 1.25 liters of high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the equisetum arvense tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Resin oak tinctures helps with eye swelling because its unique combination of plant extracts and essential oils has natural anti-inflammatory properties. The resin's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion in the eyes. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Galatella tomentosa are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties which can help soothe and reduce eye swelling.
Stems: The stems are used because they contain flavonoids which have anti-inflammatory effects that can alleviate eye swelling.
Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight infections, making them effective in treating eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried roots of galactites tomentosa at the optimal time of year.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Steep the chopped roots in 500 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid from the roots through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Filter the liquid through a 0.2 micron filter and bottle the final tincture in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and ease discomfort. The herbal extract contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, yellow gentian's diuretic properties help to remove excess fluid from the body, further alleviating eye swelling and promoting a sense of clarity and visual comfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of gentiana lutea in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full flower.

Use a ratio of 1 part roots to 2 parts solvent such as ethanol or glycerin to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the roots to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains flavonoids, terpenes, and other compounds that effectively reduce inflammation and relieve pressure on the eyes. As a natural vasodilator, it also helps to dilate blood vessels, which in turn reduces swelling and promotes healthy circulation around the delicate tissues of the eye. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lonicera japonica flowers in the morning after dewfall for optimal potency.

Combine 100g of chopped lonicera japonica flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in quantities of one pound.
Chop the flowers into small pieces and combine them with 80% vodka or glycerin.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar at room temperature for four weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from liquids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and stems selecting those with vibrant green color and no signs of decay or wilting.
Cut the plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar filling it to about one-third full.
Pour high-proof vodka over the plant material covering it completely then seal the jar tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily allowing the solvents to extract.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the plantain plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort associated with eye issues.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, making them a popular choice for treating eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of plantago major from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Weigh out 100 grams of dried plantago major herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Add 500 milliliters of vodka as a solvent and stir the mixture to combine.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily, to allow the tincture to mature.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling the product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, Prunella vulgaris, contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness around the eyes. When used as a topical application or taken orally as a tincture, selfheal's active compounds help to constrict blood vessels and soothe irritated tissues, quickly alleviating swelling and discomfort. As a natural and effective remedy, selfheal tinctures offer a gentle yet potent solution for tackling eye swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Selfheal tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can cause swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the accumulation of inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant properties: Selfheal tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and other issues.
Vasodilatory properties: Selfheal tincture's ability to dilate blood vessels helps improve blood flow to the eyes, which can reduce swelling by removing excess fluids and waste products.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of selfheal are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes.
Leaves: Selfheal leaves contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers for the tincture preparation.
Chop 20 grams of the plant material into smaller pieces to increase surface area exposure.
Combine the chopped plant material with 200 milliliters of 40% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and allow it to steep in a cool dark place for four to six weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Smooth sumac tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness in the eyes. When applied topically or consumed orally, smooth sumac tincture can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, thereby reducing eye swelling. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, further promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Sumac tincture contains compounds like gallic acid and ellagic acid that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in sumac tincture, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals that may contribute to eye swelling and damage the delicate tissues around the eyes.

Vasoconstrictive effects: Sumac tincture's ability to constrict blood vessels can help reduce swelling by reducing blood flow to the affected area, thereby alleviating puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe eye irritation.
Seeds: Contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate eye swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried rhus glabra leaves and stems for the herb material.
Combine the herb material with 80% vodka or other high-proof alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks to allow maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dark glass bottles and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and puffiness. The natural compounds present in goldenrod, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thereby alleviating eye irritation and swelling. Additionally, goldenrod's antihistamine effects help to calm down allergic reactions, further contributing to its effectiveness in reducing eye puffiness and promoting healthy, clear vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Goldenrod contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce eye swelling by decreasing inflammation and alleviating redness.

Antioxidant properties: Goldenrod's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the eyes, reducing the risk of eye swelling and promoting overall ocular health.

Mucilage content: Goldenrod contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can soothe and calm irritated tissues, including those in the eyes, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, goldenrod roots help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Flowers: Goldenrod flowers contain apigenin, a powerful antioxidant that soothes and calms irritated eye tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried solidago virgaurea flowers weighing one part by weight in a clean glass container.
Combine the solidago virgaurea flowers with 4 parts of vodka or everclear at room temperature for a minimum of two weeks.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the plant material.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dry place for three months.
Decant the tincture into smaller amber glass bottles with dropper caps using a clean pipette or measuring device.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with eye swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, including taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness in the eyes. Additionally, dandelion tincture has been used traditionally to treat a range of eye issues, from conjunctivitis to sties. By applying a few drops of the tincture under the lower eyelid or on the affected area, individuals can experience relief from eye swelling and enjoy clearer, healthier-looking eyes.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion tincture's diuretic properties help reduce fluid retention in the body, which can contribute to eye swelling by decreasing excess fluids in the eyes and surrounding tissues.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion tincture, such as sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from puffiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant Activity: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant activity helps protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and other age-related vision problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and inflammation around the eyes.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits (achenes) contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-allergic properties, making them effective in reducing eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and stems in the morning or evening when the dew is present for maximum potency.
Chop the fresh plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine 100 grams of chopped plant material with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, securing the lid tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye swelling are a natural remedy that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with puffy or swollen eyes. These liquid extracts of plants, such as peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, work by calming the skin and reducing redness, allowing for improved vision and reduced sensitivity to light. For example, peppermint tincture can be applied topically to reduce swelling and itching, while chamomile tincture helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes. This natural solution improves lives by providing a non-invasive and gentle way to address eye issues, reducing the need for harsh chemicals or prescription medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal tinctures are various skin issues, such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis. Herbal tinctures like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile extract have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe irritated skin and reduce swelling. Additionally, herbal tinctures can also be used to treat conditions like rosacea, psoriasis, and insect bites, which may exhibit similar symptoms of inflammation and redness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with eye swelling is a blend of calendula, eyebright, and chamomile. Calendula reduces inflammation and soothes the skin, while eyebright specifically addresses eye issues, reducing puffiness and redness. Chamomile calms the entire area, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can exacerbate eye swelling. Take a few drops of each tincture, mixed with water or a carrier oil, and apply it around the affected area using a cotton ball to help alleviate eye swelling and promote healing.
